About Me

Bridging computation
& living systems

I am a final-year Biomedical Engineering student at Islamic University, Kushtia, with research spanning computational cancer biology and functional biomaterials for wound healing. My work sits at the intersection of molecular simulation, materials fabrication, and clinical application.

I have co-authored research published in Computers in Biology and Medicine and Computational Biology and Chemistry (Elsevier), and presented at IEEE CS BDC Summer Symposium 2025 and IEEE COMPAS 2025. Three additional manuscripts are under review.

My final-year project develops a Janus PCL/PCL-Gelatin membrane functionalized with caffeic acid — designed for pH-responsive colorimetric infection detection and dual antibiofilm-antioxidant activity in diabetic foot ulcer care.

Beyond research, I am a trained Nazrul Geeti singer and classical dancer with First Class First certificates from Rajshahi Shilpakala Academy, and a former listed artist at Bangladesh Betar. I also teach Bangla language at Tinkers Technologies.

Research Interests

Areas of Focus

🧵
Biomaterials & Tissue Engineering

Electrospun scaffolds and smart wound dressings with stimuli-responsive behavior. Hands-on cell culture experience from internship at HGRTC, Dhaka, and the IU Biomedical Research Center.

🧬
Computational Drug Discovery

In-silico approaches including molecular docking, MD simulation, and ADMET profiling to identify and validate small-molecule inhibitors against cancer-driving protein targets such as ErbB2 and PARP1.

🔬
Cancer Biology

Targeting oncogenic signaling pathways — particularly HER2/ErbB2 and PARP1 — through in-silico and experimental approaches.

🩺
Wound Healing & Infection

Functional biomaterial solutions for chronic wound management, specifically diabetic foot ulcers with antibiofilm and antioxidant properties.

⚛️
Medical Physics

Clinical exposure to PET-CT, LINAC radiotherapy, and cyclotron radiopharmaceuticals through structured training at BAEC/INMP.

Research Focus

Current & Ongoing Projects

★ Final Year Project · 2024–2025
Janus Electrospun PCL/PCL-Gelatin Bilayer Membrane with Caffeic Acid for Diabetic Foot Ulcer Management

Novel asymmetric bilayer membrane fabricated by sequential electrospinning, functionalized with caffeic acid for pH-responsive colorimetric infection detection and dual antibiofilm-antioxidant activity. First reported caffeic acid integration in PCL/gelatin electrospun systems.

ElectrospinningPCL/GelatinCaffeic AcidpH-ResponsiveColorimetricDiabetic Wound
🧬
Computational Targeting of ErbB2 Homodimer in Breast Cancer

Molecular docking, ADMET profiling, and MD simulation evaluating N-Carbobenzoxy-L-Phenylalanine as a disruptor of ErbB2 homodimer signaling in HER2-positive breast cancer.

Molecular DockingADMETMD SimulationErbB2
⚛️
Clinical Medical Physics: PET-CT, LINAC & Radiopharmaceuticals

Structured fieldwork at INMP/BAEC covering Philips Ingenuity TF PET-CT, cyclotron radiopharmaceutical production, Varian Clinac iX LINAC, and treatment planning with AAA/Acuros XB algorithms.

PET-CTLINACRadiopharmaceuticalsTreatment Planning

Research Journey

In the Lab & Field

Cell & Tissue Culture Lab

Working at the laminar flow hood during cell and tissue culture experiments — core wet lab work at the IU Biomedical Research Center.

Biosafety Cabinet · Cell Culture Work

Sterile cell culture technique in the biosafety cabinet — hands-on wet lab training that built foundational skills in aseptic procedures and biological handling.

Biomedical Research Center · IU

Collaborative labwork with the research team at Islamic University, Kushtia.

Volunteer Research · Jaharul Haque Lab, IU

Independent bench work as volunteer research assistant — building hands-on capacity ahead of doctoral study.

HGRTC, Dhaka · Internship

Clinical observation and practical training at HGRTC, covering blood sampling and diagnostic procedures.

HGRTC, Dhaka · Internship Team

With the internship cohort at HGRTC — strengthening wet lab foundations in molecular biology and clinical diagnostics.

INMP, BAEC · Medical Physics Fieldwork

With the fieldwork group at the Institute of Nuclear Medical Physics, BAEC — covering PET-CT, LINAC, and cyclotron systems.

INMP, BAEC · Certificate of Completion

Completion certificate for "Bridging Theory and Practice: Medical Physics for Biomedical Engineering Students" — 19–23 October 2025, INMP/BAEC.

IEEE CS BDC Summer Symposium 2025

Certificate of Contribution for presenting Paper ID 17 — "N-CBZ-L-Phenylalanine Disrupts ErbB2 Homodimer Formation" — at HSTU, Bangladesh.

Arts, Culture & Craft

Nazrul Geeti Singer
Bangladesh Betar · ~4–5 Years as Listed Artist

First Class First certificate in Music from Rajshahi Shilpakala Academy (4-year formal course). Performed as a listed artist at Bangladesh Betar for four to five years, specialising in the classical repertoire of Kazi Nazrul Islam.

💃
Classical Dancer
Rajshahi Shilpakala Academy · Inter-University 2025

First Class First certificate in Dance from Rajshahi Shilpakala Academy (4-year course). Active member of Sparkle, IU's dance club — competed at the Inter-University Dance Fest at Khulna University, April 2025.

🌿
Aquascaping & Biological Design
Planted Aquarium Design · ~1 Year

Designed and maintained planted aquariums integrating aquatic ecology, aesthetics, and biological balance. Cultivated patience, observation skills, and appreciation for complex living systems — qualities that translate directly to laboratory work.
